detail: Fetched full text of the CD4+ T-cell Perturb-seq study
Located processed differential-expression matrices and code repository from the paper's data-availability section
Downloaded processed supplementary tables (DE stats, cluster annotations, autoimmune-enrichment, guide knockdown efficiency, polarization coefficients) from the study repository
Filtered the cluster autoimmune-enrichment table to multiple sclerosis; 13 regulator clusters significant at FDR<0.05, including a Th17-differentiation cluster
Located the deposited pseudobulk DE tensor in the dataset S3 bucket (33,983 perturbation-conditions x 10,282 genes; layers zscore, log_fc, adj_p_value)

Read only the eight measured-cytokine columns from the 16.8 GB HDF5 tensor by HTTP byte-range requests (no raw counts downloaded, honoring the compute-light constraint); assembled per-perturbation z and adjusted-p for CSF2, IFNG, IL2, TNF, GZMB, FOXP3, IL10, IL21
Confirmed IL-17A is not measured in this atlas (stimulation is not Th17-polarizing), fixing the encephalitogenic readout as GM-CSF + IFN-gamma
evidence: DE tensor var index: IL17A absent; CSF2/IFNG present

detail: Fixed the pathogenic-effector set (core CSF2, IFNG) and confound sets (IL2/activation, proliferation, stress) from independent references before ranking
Verified negative z = knockdown lowers the cytokine, using canonical positive regulators (TBX21, STAT3, LAT/LCP2/ZAP70) whose knockdown gave strongly negative cytokine z-scores
Scored all 11,526 knockdowns on effector suppression (GM-CSF+IFN-gamma) versus activation (IL-2) to separate pan-activation hits from effector-selective hits

Excluded TCR-proximal/pan-activation, general-transcription/chromatin, essential genes, and the cytokines themselves; 195 rows (166 genes) passed the significance+reproducibility gate, 143 survived confound filters

Nominated PKM (PKM2): effector-selective (GM-CSF z=-3.66, IFN-gamma z=-3.30, IL-2 z=-0.05), selectivity 3.4, on-target -32.2, cross-donor 0.78, specific to 8 h; among glycolytic genes only PKM and PGK1 show the effect
Queried the GWAS Catalog for PKM associations; found no genome-wide-significant PKM-locus association with MS or CNS autoimmunity (hits are metabolic/anthropometric)
evidence: GWAS Catalog, gene=PKM (9 associations, none MS/CNS)

detail: Established that nuclear PKM2 dimer binds STAT3 to amplify effector cytokines, and that T-cell PKM2 deletion ameliorates EAE without impairing proliferation — explaining the observed effector-selectivity
Confirmed PKM2 is requisite for Th1/Th17 differentiation, is bound/activated by CaMK4 downstream of the TCR, and that a PKM2 inhibitor ameliorates EAE
Determined the corrective direction is to stabilize the PKM2 tetramer and block nuclear translocation, using the tetramer activator TEPP-46 which limits Th1/Th17 and suppresses EAE in vivo

Identified micheliolide as the natural metabolite that covalently binds PKM2 at Cys424 (absent in PKM1), promotes the tetramer, inhibits K433 acetylation and blocks nuclear translocation — direction-matched to the required move
Confirmed the parent sesquiterpene-lactone parthenolide suppresses Th17 and alleviates EAE, and that micheliolide is more stable in vivo than parthenolide
Verified the feverfew parent parthenolide is a natural product that reached clinical Phase 2 and anchors the covalent alpha-methylene-gamma-butyrolactone chemistry

Established the oral prodrug ACT001 has ~51% bioavailability and crosses the blood-brain barrier in rats; flagged that unbound CNS-available immunomodulatory concentration at tolerated human doses is unestablished

Checked ClinicalTrials.gov for the prodrug: one Phase 1b/2a glioblastoma trial active/not-recruiting and one STAT3-high glioblastoma Phase 2 trial suspended, both with no results posted (tolerability context only)

Confirmed a human MS study showing effector/memory T cells over-express PKM2, MS PBMCs carry more PKM2 monomer, and PKM2 targeting preferentially inhibits IFN-gamma/IL-5/IL-13/IL-17; full text confirms the compound used is TEPP-46, not micheliolide

Resolved direction-of-effect across perturbation modalities (gene CRISPRi, genetic deletion, reversible activator, covalent activator, clinical prodrug) rather than merging them, to make explicit what the pilot must still establish

Designed a 2x2 compound x CRISPRi-PKM2 factorial epistasis pilot in primary human CD4+ T cells; primary endpoint GM-CSF+IFN-gamma+ frequency at 8 h; mechanism-defining result is a drug x genotype interaction; added isoform-attribution/rescue, a multi-day polarization arm, target-engagement and off-target controls, and a donor-as-unit mixed-effects analysis
Resolved every PMID to its DOI via NCBI and read each abstract to confirm the paper supports the exact claim attached to it; reconciled all dataset numbers against the extracted source table
evidence: NCBI ID conversion + article metadata; cytokine_effects.parquet

detail: Rendered the publication PDF with the three figures and two tables embedded, and assembled the presentation folder
